Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple Basic attackers targeting transportation systems may use drive-by compromises, exploit public-facing applications, and leverage custom command and control protocols to disrupt operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Active Directory, Network Traffic, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Custom Command and Control Protocol, Command Execution (Mechanism), Network Traffic Content (Mechanism), File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker uses public Wi-Fi or unsecured networks near transportation hubs to compromise devices through drive-by downloads.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker exploits known vulnerabilities in outdated software used by transportation systems.

stage.name: Execution
stage.technique_name: T1094: Custom Command and Control Protocol
stage.description: The attacker establishes a C2 server using compromised devices or cloud services.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: The attacker uses simple scripting languages like Python or PowerShell to automate tasks.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker overloads transportation systems with traffic or data requests to cause disruptions.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attacker deletes or corrupts critical system files to cause long-term damage.

Scenario.summary: This scenario outlines a basic attack plan targeting transportation systems, utilizing drive-by compromises, exploiting public-facing applications, and leveraging custom command and control protocols to achieve disruption.
